Backend Programming Notes for BE Role (Part 3)

Focus: Advanced Concurrency, Locking Mechanisms, High Traffic Patterns

Table of Contents

  1. Advanced Concurrency
    • Fork/Join Framework
    • Lock-Free Programming
    • CAS and ABA Problem
    • Memory Barriers & Happens-Before
  2. Advanced Locking
    • Lock Striping
    • Distributed Lock Algorithms
    • Database Locking
    • Spin Lock vs Mutex
  3. High Traffic Patterns
    • Request Coalescing
    • Load Shedding
    • Bulkhead Pattern
    • Hot Key Problem
    • Connection Pool Tuning
    • Consistent Hashing
  4. Real-World Scenarios
  5. Common Interview Questions

Advanced Concurrency

1. Fork/Join Framework

1import java.util.concurrent.*;
2
3/**
4 * Fork/Join: Divide-and-conquer parallelism with work stealing.
5 * Ideal for recursive algorithms that can be split into subtasks.
6 */
7public class ForkJoinDemo {
8
9    private static final ForkJoinPool pool = ForkJoinPool.commonPool();
10
11    // Example: Parallel sum of array
12    static class SumTask extends RecursiveTask<Long> {
13        private static final int THRESHOLD = 10_000;
14        private final long[] array;
15        private final int start, end;
16
17        SumTask(long[] array, int start, int end) {
18            this.array = array;
19            this.start = start;
20            this.end = end;
21        }
22
23        @Override
24        protected Long compute() {
25            int length = end - start;
26
27            // Base case: compute directly
28            if (length <= THRESHOLD) {
29                long sum = 0;
30                for (int i = start; i < end; i++) {
31                    sum += array[i];
32                }
33                return sum;
34            }
35
36            // Recursive case: split and fork
37            int mid = start + length / 2;
38            SumTask leftTask = new SumTask(array, start, mid);
39            SumTask rightTask = new SumTask(array, mid, end);
40
41            // Fork left task (async)
42            leftTask.fork();
43
44            // Compute right task in current thread
45            long rightResult = rightTask.compute();
46
47            // Join left task result
48            long leftResult = leftTask.join();
49
50            return leftResult + rightResult;
51        }
52    }
53
54    // Example: Parallel merge sort
55    static class MergeSortTask extends RecursiveAction {
56        private static final int THRESHOLD = 1000;
57        private final int[] array;
58        private final int start, end;
59        private final int[] temp;
60
61        MergeSortTask(int[] array, int start, int end, int[] temp) {
62            this.array = array;
63            this.start = start;
64            this.end = end;
65            this.temp = temp;
66        }
67
68        @Override
69        protected void compute() {
70            if (end - start <= THRESHOLD) {
71                // Use sequential sort for small arrays
72                java.util.Arrays.sort(array, start, end);
73                return;
74            }
75
76            int mid = start + (end - start) / 2;
77
78            // Fork both subtasks
79            MergeSortTask left = new MergeSortTask(array, start, mid, temp);
80            MergeSortTask right = new MergeSortTask(array, mid, end, temp);
81
82            invokeAll(left, right);
83
84            // Merge results
85            merge(array, start, mid, end, temp);
86        }
87
88        private void merge(int[] arr, int start, int mid, int end, int[] temp) {
89            System.arraycopy(arr, start, temp, start, end - start);
90
91            int i = start, j = mid, k = start;
92            while (i < mid && j < end) {
93                arr[k++] = temp[i] <= temp[j] ? temp[i++] : temp[j++];
94            }
95            while (i < mid) arr[k++] = temp[i++];
96        }
97    }
98
99    public static void main(String[] args) {
100        long[] array = new long[1_000_000];
101        for (int i = 0; i < array.length; i++) array[i] = i;
102
103        long sum = pool.invoke(new SumTask(array, 0, array.length));
104        System.out.println("Sum: " + sum);
105    }
106}

2. Lock-Free Programming

1import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
2
3/**
4 * Lock-free stack using CAS (Compare-And-Swap).
5 * No locks, but uses atomic operations for thread safety.
6 */
7public class LockFreeStack<T> {
8
9    private final AtomicReference<Node<T>> top = new AtomicReference<>();
10
11    private static class Node<T> {
12        final T value;
13        final Node<T> next;
14
15        Node(T value, Node<T> next) {
16            this.value = value;
17            this.next = next;
18        }
19    }
20
21    public void push(T value) {
22        Node<T> newNode = new Node<>(value, null);
23
24        while (true) {
25            Node<T> currentTop = top.get();
26            newNode = new Node<>(value, currentTop);
27
28            // CAS: if top is still currentTop, set it to newNode
29            if (top.compareAndSet(currentTop, newNode)) {
30                return; // Success
31            }
32            // Else: another thread modified top, retry
33        }
34    }
35
36    public T pop() {
37        while (true) {
38            Node<T> currentTop = top.get();
39
40            if (currentTop == null) {
41                return null; // Stack is empty
42            }
43
44            Node<T> newTop = currentTop.next;
45
46            // CAS: if top is still currentTop, set it to newTop
47            if (top.compareAndSet(currentTop, newTop)) {
48                return currentTop.value; // Success
49            }
50            // Else: retry
51        }
52    }
53
54    public T peek() {
55        Node<T> currentTop = top.get();
56        return currentTop != null ? currentTop.value : null;
57    }
58
59    public boolean isEmpty() {
60        return top.get() == null;
61    }
62}
63
64/**
65 * Lock-free counter using CAS with backoff.
66 */
67public class LockFreeCounter {
68
69    private final AtomicLong value = new AtomicLong(0);
70
71    public long incrementAndGet() {
72        while (true) {
73            long current = value.get();
74            long next = current + 1;
75
76            if (value.compareAndSet(current, next)) {
77                return next;
78            }
79            // Backoff on contention
80            Thread.onSpinWait();
81        }
82    }
83
84    // Better: use built-in atomic increment
85    public long incrementAndGetSimple() {
86        return value.incrementAndGet();
87    }
88
89    public long get() {
90        return value.get();
91    }
92}

3. CAS and ABA Problem

1import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
2
3/**
4 * ABA Problem: Value changes A -> B -> A, CAS succeeds but state changed.
5 * Solution: Use AtomicStampedReference (version number).
6 */
7public class ABADemo {
8
9    // PROBLEM: ABA issue with AtomicReference
10    static class BrokenStack<T> {
11        private final AtomicReference<Node<T>> top = new AtomicReference<>();
12
13        static class Node<T> {
14            T value;
15            Node<T> next;
16            Node(T v, Node<T> n) { value = v; next = n; }
17        }
18
19        /*
20         * ABA Scenario:
21         * 1. Thread A reads top = [A -> B -> C]
22         * 2. Thread A is suspended
23         * 3. Thread B pops A, pops B, pushes D, pushes A
24         * 4. top = [A -> D -> C]  (A is reused but different state!)
25         * 5. Thread A resumes, CAS(A, B) succeeds (A == A)
26         * 6. But now top = [B] with B pointing to freed memory or wrong node
27         */
28    }
29
30    // SOLUTION: AtomicStampedReference with version
31    static class SafeStack<T> {
32        private final AtomicStampedReference<Node<T>> top =
33            new AtomicStampedReference<>(null, 0);
34
35        static class Node<T> {
36            T value;
37            Node<T> next;
38            Node(T v, Node<T> n) { value = v; next = n; }
39        }
40
41        public void push(T value) {
42            int[] stampHolder = new int[1];
43
44            while (true) {
45                Node<T> currentTop = top.get(stampHolder);
46                int currentStamp = stampHolder[0];
47
48                Node<T> newNode = new Node<>(value, currentTop);
49
50                // CAS with stamp - detects ABA
51                if (top.compareAndSet(currentTop, newNode, currentStamp, currentStamp + 1)) {
52                    return;
53                }
54            }
55        }
56
57        public T pop() {
58            int[] stampHolder = new int[1];
59
60            while (true) {
61                Node<T> currentTop = top.get(stampHolder);
62                int currentStamp = stampHolder[0];
63
64                if (currentTop == null) {
65                    return null;
66                }
67
68                Node<T> newTop = currentTop.next;
69
70                // CAS with stamp - detects ABA
71                if (top.compareAndSet(currentTop, newTop, currentStamp, currentStamp + 1)) {
72                    return currentTop.value;
73                }
74            }
75        }
76    }
77
78    // Alternative: AtomicMarkableReference for simpler mark/unmark
79    static class MarkableDemo {
80        private final AtomicMarkableReference<String> ref =
81            new AtomicMarkableReference<>("initial", false);
82
83        public void markAsDeleted() {
84            while (true) {
85                String current = ref.getReference();
86                if (ref.compareAndSet(current, current, false, true)) {
87                    return;
88                }
89            }
90        }
91
92        public boolean isMarked() {
93            return ref.isMarked();
94        }
95    }
96}

4. Memory Barriers & Happens-Before

1/**
2 * Java Memory Model (JMM) guarantees with happens-before relationships.
3 */
4public class MemoryBarrierDemo {
5
6    // WRONG: No visibility guarantee
7    static class BrokenVisibility {
8        private boolean flag = false;
9        private int value = 0;
10
11        public void writer() {
12            value = 42;
13            flag = true;  // Might be reordered before value = 42
14        }
15
16        public void reader() {
17            if (flag) {
18                System.out.println(value);  // Might print 0!
19            }
20        }
21    }
22
23    // CORRECT: volatile establishes happens-before
24    static class VolatileVisibility {
25        private volatile boolean flag = false;
26        private int value = 0;
27
28        public void writer() {
29            value = 42;
30            flag = true;  // volatile write - memory barrier
31        }
32
33        public void reader() {
34            if (flag) {  // volatile read - memory barrier
35                System.out.println(value);  // Guaranteed to print 42
36            }
37        }
38    }
39
40    // CORRECT: synchronized establishes happens-before
41    static class SynchronizedVisibility {
42        private boolean flag = false;
43        private int value = 0;
44        private final Object lock = new Object();
45
46        public void writer() {
47            synchronized (lock) {
48                value = 42;
49                flag = true;
50            }  // Release lock - memory barrier
51        }
52
53        public void reader() {
54            synchronized (lock) {  // Acquire lock - memory barrier
55                if (flag) {
56                    System.out.println(value);  // Guaranteed to print 42
57                }
58            }
59        }
60    }
61
62    /**
63     * Happens-Before Rules:
64     * 1. Program order: Each action in a thread happens-before every subsequent action
65     * 2. Monitor lock: unlock() happens-before subsequent lock()
66     * 3. Volatile: write happens-before subsequent read
67     * 4. Thread start: Thread.start() happens-before any action in started thread
68     * 5. Thread join: All actions in thread happen-before join() returns
69     * 6. Transitivity: If A happens-before B and B happens-before C, then A happens-before C
70     */
71}

Advanced Locking

1. Lock Striping

1import java.util.concurrent.locks.*;
2
3/**
4 * Lock striping: Divide data into segments, each with its own lock.
5 * Reduces contention compared to single global lock.
6 */
7public class StripedMap<K, V> {
8
9    private static final int DEFAULT_STRIPES = 16;
10
11    private final int numStripes;
12    private final Node<K, V>[] buckets;
13    private final ReentrantLock[] locks;
14
15    @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
16    public StripedMap(int numBuckets, int numStripes) {
17        this.numStripes = numStripes;
18        this.buckets = new Node[numBuckets];
19        this.locks = new ReentrantLock[numStripes];
20
21        for (int i = 0; i < numStripes; i++) {
22            locks[i] = new ReentrantLock();
23        }
24    }
25
26    private static class Node<K, V> {
27        final K key;
28        V value;
29        Node<K, V> next;
30
31        Node(K key, V value, Node<K, V> next) {
32            this.key = key;
33            this.value = value;
34            this.next = next;
35        }
36    }
37
38    private int getBucketIndex(K key) {
39        return Math.abs(key.hashCode() % buckets.length);
40    }
41
42    private int getStripeIndex(K key) {
43        return Math.abs(key.hashCode() % numStripes);
44    }
45
46    public V get(K key) {
47        int bucketIndex = getBucketIndex(key);
48        int stripeIndex = getStripeIndex(key);
49
50        locks[stripeIndex].lock();
51        try {
52            Node<K, V> node = buckets[bucketIndex];
53            while (node != null) {
54                if (node.key.equals(key)) {
55                    return node.value;
56                }
57                node = node.next;
58            }
59            return null;
60        } finally {
61            locks[stripeIndex].unlock();
62        }
63    }
64
65    public void put(K key, V value) {
66        int bucketIndex = getBucketIndex(key);
67        int stripeIndex = getStripeIndex(key);
68
69        locks[stripeIndex].lock();
70        try {
71            Node<K, V> node = buckets[bucketIndex];
72
73            // Check if key exists
74            while (node != null) {
75                if (node.key.equals(key)) {
76                    node.value = value;
77                    return;
78                }
79                node = node.next;
80            }
81
82            // Add new node
83            buckets[bucketIndex] = new Node<>(key, value, buckets[bucketIndex]);
84        } finally {
85            locks[stripeIndex].unlock();
86        }
87    }
88
89    // For operations requiring all locks (resize, clear, etc.)
90    public void clear() {
91        for (int i = 0; i < numStripes; i++) {
92            locks[i].lock();
93        }
94        try {
95            for (int i = 0; i < buckets.length; i++) {
96                buckets[i] = null;
97            }
98        } finally {
99            for (int i = numStripes - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
100                locks[i].unlock();
101            }
102        }
103    }
104}

2. Distributed Lock Algorithms

Redlock (Redis)

1import redis.clients.jedis.Jedis;
2import redis.clients.jedis.params.SetParams;
3import java.util.*;
4
5/**
6 * Redlock: Distributed lock using multiple Redis instances.
7 * Requires majority (N/2 + 1) of nodes to agree.
8 */
9public class RedlockDistributedLock {
10
11    private final List<Jedis> redisNodes;
12    private final int quorum;
13    private final long lockTtlMs;
14    private final long retryDelayMs;
15    private final int maxRetries;
16
17    public RedlockDistributedLock(List<Jedis> redisNodes, long lockTtlMs) {
18        this.redisNodes = redisNodes;
19        this.quorum = redisNodes.size() / 2 + 1;  // Majority
20        this.lockTtlMs = lockTtlMs;
21        this.retryDelayMs = 200;
22        this.maxRetries = 3;
23    }
24
25    public LockResult tryLock(String resource) {
26        String lockValue = UUID.randomUUID().toString();
27        String lockKey = "lock:" + resource;
28
29        for (int retry = 0; retry < maxRetries; retry++) {
30            long startTime = System.currentTimeMillis();
31            int acquiredCount = 0;
32            List<Jedis> acquiredNodes = new ArrayList<>();
33
34            // Try to acquire lock on all nodes
35            for (Jedis node : redisNodes) {
36                try {
37                    String result = node.set(
38                        lockKey, lockValue,
39                        SetParams.setParams().nx().px(lockTtlMs)
40                    );
41                    if ("OK".equals(result)) {
42                        acquiredCount++;
43                        acquiredNodes.add(node);
44                    }
45                } catch (Exception e) {
46                    // Node unavailable - continue
47                }
48            }
49
50            // Calculate elapsed time
51            long elapsedMs = System.currentTimeMillis() - startTime;
52
53            // Check if we acquired quorum within valid time
54            // (lock must be valid for at least some minimum time)
55            long validityTime = lockTtlMs - elapsedMs - 2; // drift factor
56
57            if (acquiredCount >= quorum && validityTime > 0) {
58                return new LockResult(true, lockValue, validityTime);
59            }
60
61            // Failed - release any acquired locks
62            for (Jedis node : acquiredNodes) {
63                releaseLock(node, lockKey, lockValue);
64            }
65
66            // Wait before retry
67            try {
68                Thread.sleep(retryDelayMs + (long)(Math.random() * retryDelayMs));
69            } catch (InterruptedException e) {
70                Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
71                return new LockResult(false, null, 0);
72            }
73        }
74
75        return new LockResult(false, null, 0);
76    }
77
78    public void unlock(String resource, String lockValue) {
79        String lockKey = "lock:" + resource;
80
81        for (Jedis node : redisNodes) {
82            try {
83                releaseLock(node, lockKey, lockValue);
84            } catch (Exception e) {
85                // Continue releasing on other nodes
86            }
87        }
88    }
89
90    private void releaseLock(Jedis node, String lockKey, String lockValue) {
91        // Lua script: only delete if value matches
92        String script =
93            "if redis.call('get', KEYS[1]) == ARGV[1] then " +
94            "    return redis.call('del', KEYS[1]) " +
95            "else " +
96            "    return 0 " +
97            "end";
98
99        node.eval(script,
100            Collections.singletonList(lockKey),
101            Collections.singletonList(lockValue));
102    }
103
104    public static class LockResult {
105        public final boolean acquired;
106        public final String lockValue;
107        public final long validityTimeMs;
108
109        LockResult(boolean acquired, String lockValue, long validityTimeMs) {
110            this.acquired = acquired;
111            this.lockValue = lockValue;
112            this.validityTimeMs = validityTimeMs;
113        }
114    }
115}

ZooKeeper Lock

1import org.apache.zookeeper.*;
2import java.util.*;
3import java.util.concurrent.CountDownLatch;
4
5/**
6 * Distributed lock using ZooKeeper ephemeral sequential nodes.
7 * Fair lock: Requests are processed in order.
8 */
9public class ZooKeeperDistributedLock {
10
11    private final ZooKeeper zk;
12    private final String lockPath;
13    private String currentLockNode;
14
15    public ZooKeeperDistributedLock(ZooKeeper zk, String lockPath) {
16        this.zk = zk;
17        this.lockPath = lockPath;
18    }
19
20    public void lock() throws Exception {
21        // Create ephemeral sequential node
22        currentLockNode = zk.create(
23            lockPath + "/lock_",
24            new byte[0],
25            ZooDefs.Ids.OPEN_ACL_UNSAFE,
26            CreateMode.EPHEMERAL_SEQUENTIAL
27        );
28
29        while (true) {
30            // Get all children and sort
31            List<String> children = zk.getChildren(lockPath, false);
32            Collections.sort(children);
33
34            String currentNodeName = currentLockNode.substring(lockPath.length() + 1);
35            int currentIndex = children.indexOf(currentNodeName);
36
37            if (currentIndex == 0) {
38                // We have the lock!
39                return;
40            }
41
42            // Watch the previous node
43            String prevNode = children.get(currentIndex - 1);
44            CountDownLatch latch = new CountDownLatch(1);
45
46            Stat stat = zk.exists(lockPath + "/" + prevNode, event -> {
47                if (event.getType() == Watcher.Event.EventType.NodeDeleted) {
48                    latch.countDown();
49                }
50            });
51
52            if (stat != null) {
53                // Wait for previous node to be deleted
54                latch.await();
55            }
56            // Else: previous node already deleted, retry loop
57        }
58    }
59
60    public void unlock() throws Exception {
61        if (currentLockNode != null) {
62            zk.delete(currentLockNode, -1);
63            currentLockNode = null;
64        }
65    }
66
67    // Try lock with timeout
68    public boolean tryLock(long timeoutMs) throws Exception {
69        long deadline = System.currentTimeMillis() + timeoutMs;
70
71        currentLockNode = zk.create(
72            lockPath + "/lock_",
73            new byte[0],
74            ZooDefs.Ids.OPEN_ACL_UNSAFE,
75            CreateMode.EPHEMERAL_SEQUENTIAL
76        );
77
78        while (System.currentTimeMillis() < deadline) {
79            List<String> children = zk.getChildren(lockPath, false);
80            Collections.sort(children);
81
82            String currentNodeName = currentLockNode.substring(lockPath.length() + 1);
83            int currentIndex = children.indexOf(currentNodeName);
84
85            if (currentIndex == 0) {
86                return true;
87            }
88
89            String prevNode = children.get(currentIndex - 1);
90            CountDownLatch latch = new CountDownLatch(1);
91
92            Stat stat = zk.exists(lockPath + "/" + prevNode, event -> {
93                latch.countDown();
94            });
95
96            if (stat != null) {
97                long remainingMs = deadline - System.currentTimeMillis();
98                if (remainingMs > 0) {
99                    latch.await(remainingMs, java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);
100                }
101            }
102        }
103
104        // Timeout - cleanup
105        unlock();
106        return false;
107    }
108}

3. Database Locking

1/**
2 * Database locking strategies.
3 */
4public class DatabaseLockingDemo {
5
6    // Pessimistic Locking: SELECT FOR UPDATE
7    public Order getOrderWithLock(Connection conn, long orderId) throws SQLException {
8        String sql = "SELECT * FROM orders WHERE id = ? FOR UPDATE";
9
10        try (PreparedStatement stmt = conn.prepareStatement(sql)) {
11            stmt.setLong(1, orderId);
12            ResultSet rs = stmt.executeQuery();
13
14            if (rs.next()) {
15                return mapToOrder(rs);
16            }
17            return null;
18        }
19        // Lock released when transaction commits/rollbacks
20    }
21
22    // Pessimistic with NOWAIT (fail immediately if locked)
23    public Order getOrderWithLockNoWait(Connection conn, long orderId) throws SQLException {
24        String sql = "SELECT * FROM orders WHERE id = ? FOR UPDATE NOWAIT";
25        // Throws exception if row is locked
26        // ...
27        return null;
28    }
29
30    // Pessimistic with SKIP LOCKED (skip locked rows)
31    public List<Order> getUnlockedOrders(Connection conn, int limit) throws SQLException {
32        String sql = "SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'PENDING' " +
33                     "FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT ?";
34        // Returns only unlocked rows - useful for job processing
35        // ...
36        return null;
37    }
38
39    // Optimistic Locking: Version column
40    public boolean updateOrderOptimistic(Connection conn, Order order) throws SQLException {
41        String sql = "UPDATE orders SET status = ?, version = version + 1 " +
42                     "WHERE id = ? AND version = ?";
43
44        try (PreparedStatement stmt = conn.prepareStatement(sql)) {
45            stmt.setString(1, order.getStatus());
46            stmt.setLong(2, order.getId());
47            stmt.setInt(3, order.getVersion());
48
49            int updated = stmt.executeUpdate();
50
51            if (updated == 0) {
52                // Version mismatch - another transaction modified it
53                throw new OptimisticLockException("Order was modified by another transaction");
54            }
55            return true;
56        }
57    }
58
59    // Advisory Lock (PostgreSQL)
60    public void withAdvisoryLock(Connection conn, long lockId, Runnable action)
61            throws SQLException {
62
63        try (PreparedStatement lock = conn.prepareStatement("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(?)")) {
64            lock.setLong(1, lockId);
65            lock.execute();
66
67            try {
68                action.run();
69            } finally {
70                try (PreparedStatement unlock = conn.prepareStatement(
71                        "SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(?)")) {
72                    unlock.setLong(1, lockId);
73                    unlock.execute();
74                }
75            }
76        }
77    }
78
79    // Advisory Lock with timeout (PostgreSQL)
80    public boolean tryAdvisoryLock(Connection conn, long lockId, long timeoutMs)
81            throws SQLException {
82
83        String sql = "SELECT pg_try_advisory_lock(?)";
84        try (PreparedStatement stmt = conn.prepareStatement(sql)) {
85            stmt.setLong(1, lockId);
86            ResultSet rs = stmt.executeQuery();
87            rs.next();
88            return rs.getBoolean(1);
89        }
90    }
91
92    private Order mapToOrder(ResultSet rs) throws SQLException {
93        return new Order(); // mapping logic
94    }
95
96    static class OptimisticLockException extends RuntimeException {
97        OptimisticLockException(String msg) { super(msg); }
98    }
99}

4. Spin Lock vs Mutex

1import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
2
3/**
4 * Spin Lock: Busy-wait loop, good for short critical sections.
5 */
6public class SpinLock {
7
8    private final AtomicBoolean locked = new AtomicBoolean(false);
9
10    // Basic spin lock
11    public void lock() {
12        while (!locked.compareAndSet(false, true)) {
13            // Busy wait
14        }
15    }
16
17    // Spin lock with exponential backoff
18    public void lockWithBackoff() {
19        int backoff = 1;
20        int maxBackoff = 1000;
21
22        while (!locked.compareAndSet(false, true)) {
23            // Backoff
24            for (int i = 0; i < backoff; i++) {
25                Thread.onSpinWait();
26            }
27            backoff = Math.min(backoff * 2, maxBackoff);
28        }
29    }
30
31    // Spin lock with yield
32    public void lockWithYield() {
33        while (!locked.compareAndSet(false, true)) {
34            Thread.yield();  // Give up CPU time slice
35        }
36    }
37
38    public void unlock() {
39        locked.set(false);
40    }
41
42    public boolean tryLock() {
43        return locked.compareAndSet(false, true);
44    }
45}
46
47/**
48 * Ticket Lock: Fair spin lock with FIFO ordering.
49 */
50public class TicketLock {
51
52    private final AtomicLong ticketCounter = new AtomicLong(0);
53    private final AtomicLong nowServing = new AtomicLong(0);
54
55    public void lock() {
56        long myTicket = ticketCounter.getAndIncrement();
57
58        while (nowServing.get() != myTicket) {
59            Thread.onSpinWait();
60        }
61    }
62
63    public void unlock() {
64        nowServing.incrementAndGet();
65    }
66}
67
68/**
69 * CLH Lock: Queue-based fair lock with better cache behavior.
70 */
71public class CLHLock {
72
73    private final AtomicReference<Node> tail = new AtomicReference<>(new Node());
74    private final ThreadLocal<Node> myNode = ThreadLocal.withInitial(Node::new);
75    private final ThreadLocal<Node> myPred = new ThreadLocal<>();
76
77    private static class Node {
78        volatile boolean locked = false;
79    }
80
81    public void lock() {
82        Node node = myNode.get();
83        node.locked = true;
84
85        // Add to queue
86        Node pred = tail.getAndSet(node);
87        myPred.set(pred);
88
89        // Spin on predecessor's lock
90        while (pred.locked) {
91            Thread.onSpinWait();
92        }
93    }
94
95    public void unlock() {
96        Node node = myNode.get();
97        node.locked = false;
98
99        // Reuse predecessor's node
100        myNode.set(myPred.get());
101    }
102}

Comparison:

Aspect Spin Lock Mutex
Wait strategy Busy-wait (CPU spinning) Block (OS context switch)
Best for Very short critical sections Long critical sections
CPU usage High when contended Low when waiting
Latency Low (no context switch) Higher (context switch)
Fairness Usually unfair Can be fair
Use case Low-level, real-time General purpose

High Traffic Patterns

1. Request Coalescing

1import java.util.concurrent.*;
2
3/**
4 * Request Coalescing: Multiple identical requests share one backend call.
5 * Prevents thundering herd problem.
6 */
7public class RequestCoalescer<K, V> {
8
9    private final ConcurrentHashMap<K, CompletableFuture<V>> inFlightRequests =
10        new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
11
12    private final Function<K, V> fetchFunction;
13    private final ExecutorService executor;
14
15    public RequestCoalescer(Function<K, V> fetchFunction, ExecutorService executor) {
16        this.fetchFunction = fetchFunction;
17        this.executor = executor;
18    }
19
20    public CompletableFuture<V> get(K key) {
21        // Try to join existing request or create new one
22        return inFlightRequests.computeIfAbsent(key, k -> {
23            CompletableFuture<V> future = CompletableFuture.supplyAsync(
24                () -> fetchFunction.apply(k),
25                executor
26            );
27
28            // Remove from map when complete
29            future.whenComplete((result, ex) -> inFlightRequests.remove(k));
30
31            return future;
32        });
33    }
34
35    // With timeout for stale entries
36    public CompletableFuture<V> getWithTimeout(K key, long timeoutMs) {
37        return get(key).orTimeout(timeoutMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);
38    }
39}
40
41/**
42 * Batch Coalescer: Collect multiple requests into single batch call.
43 */
44public class BatchCoalescer<K, V> {
45
46    private final int batchSize;
47    private final long maxWaitMs;
48    private final Function<List<K>, Map<K, V>> batchFunction;
49
50    private final ConcurrentHashMap<K, CompletableFuture<V>> pendingRequests =
51        new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
52    private final BlockingQueue<K> requestQueue = new LinkedBlockingQueue<>();
53    private final ScheduledExecutorService scheduler = Executors.newSingleThreadScheduledExecutor();
54
55    public BatchCoalescer(int batchSize, long maxWaitMs,
56                          Function<List<K>, Map<K, V>> batchFunction) {
57        this.batchSize = batchSize;
58        this.maxWaitMs = maxWaitMs;
59        this.batchFunction = batchFunction;
60
61        startBatchProcessor();
62    }
63
64    public CompletableFuture<V> get(K key) {
65        return pendingRequests.computeIfAbsent(key, k -> {
66            requestQueue.offer(k);
67            return new CompletableFuture<>();
68        });
69    }
70
71    private void startBatchProcessor() {
72        scheduler.scheduleWithFixedDelay(() -> {
73            List<K> batch = new ArrayList<>();
74            requestQueue.drainTo(batch, batchSize);
75
76            if (!batch.isEmpty()) {
77                try {
78                    Map<K, V> results = batchFunction.apply(batch);
79
80                    for (K key : batch) {
81                        CompletableFuture<V> future = pendingRequests.remove(key);
82                        if (future != null) {
83                            V value = results.get(key);
84                            if (value != null) {
85                                future.complete(value);
86                            } else {
87                                future.completeExceptionally(
88                                    new RuntimeException("Not found: " + key));
89                            }
90                        }
91                    }
92                } catch (Exception e) {
93                    for (K key : batch) {
94                        CompletableFuture<V> future = pendingRequests.remove(key);
95                        if (future != null) {
96                            future.completeExceptionally(e);
97                        }
98                    }
99                }
100            }
101        }, 0, maxWaitMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);
102    }
103}

2. Load Shedding

1import java.util.concurrent.*;
2import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
3
4/**
5 * Load Shedding: Reject excess requests to maintain service quality.
6 */
7public class LoadShedder {
8
9    private final int maxConcurrent;
10    private final AtomicInteger currentLoad = new AtomicInteger(0);
11    private final double shedThreshold;  // Start shedding at this percentage
12
13    public LoadShedder(int maxConcurrent, double shedThreshold) {
14        this.maxConcurrent = maxConcurrent;
15        this.shedThreshold = shedThreshold;
16    }
17
18    public <T> T execute(Callable<T> task) throws Exception {
19        int current = currentLoad.get();
20
21        // Hard limit
22        if (current >= maxConcurrent) {
23            throw new LoadSheddingException("System overloaded");
24        }
25
26        // Probabilistic shedding near threshold
27        double loadPercentage = (double) current / maxConcurrent;
28        if (loadPercentage > shedThreshold) {
29            double shedProbability = (loadPercentage - shedThreshold) / (1 - shedThreshold);
30            if (Math.random() < shedProbability) {
31                throw new LoadSheddingException("Request shed due to high load");
32            }
33        }
34
35        currentLoad.incrementAndGet();
36        try {
37            return task.call();
38        } finally {
39            currentLoad.decrementAndGet();
40        }
41    }
42
43    public static class LoadSheddingException extends RuntimeException {
44        LoadSheddingException(String msg) { super(msg); }
45    }
46}
47
48/**
49 * Adaptive Load Shedder: Adjusts based on latency.
50 */
51public class AdaptiveLoadShedder {
52
53    private final int maxConcurrent;
54    private final long targetLatencyMs;
55    private final AtomicInteger currentLoad = new AtomicInteger(0);
56    private final AtomicLong avgLatencyMs = new AtomicLong(0);
57
58    public AdaptiveLoadShedder(int maxConcurrent, long targetLatencyMs) {
59        this.maxConcurrent = maxConcurrent;
60        this.targetLatencyMs = targetLatencyMs;
61    }
62
63    public <T> T execute(Callable<T> task) throws Exception {
64        // Shed if latency is high, regardless of concurrent count
65        if (avgLatencyMs.get() > targetLatencyMs * 2 && currentLoad.get() > maxConcurrent / 2) {
66            throw new LoadSheddingException("Shedding due to high latency");
67        }
68
69        if (currentLoad.get() >= maxConcurrent) {
70            throw new LoadSheddingException("Max concurrent reached");
71        }
72
73        currentLoad.incrementAndGet();
74        long start = System.currentTimeMillis();
75
76        try {
77            return task.call();
78        } finally {
79            long latency = System.currentTimeMillis() - start;
80            updateLatency(latency);
81            currentLoad.decrementAndGet();
82        }
83    }
84
85    private void updateLatency(long latency) {
86        // Exponential moving average
87        avgLatencyMs.updateAndGet(avg -> (avg * 9 + latency) / 10);
88    }
89
90    public static class LoadSheddingException extends RuntimeException {
91        LoadSheddingException(String msg) { super(msg); }
92    }
93}

3. Bulkhead Pattern

1import java.util.concurrent.*;
2
3/**
4 * Bulkhead: Isolate failures to prevent cascading.
5 * Each service/operation has its own resource pool.
6 */
7public class BulkheadManager {
8
9    private final ConcurrentHashMap<String, Bulkhead> bulkheads = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
10
11    public Bulkhead getBulkhead(String name, int maxConcurrent, int queueSize) {
12        return bulkheads.computeIfAbsent(name,
13            n -> new Bulkhead(n, maxConcurrent, queueSize));
14    }
15
16    public static class Bulkhead {
17        private final String name;
18        private final Semaphore semaphore;
19        private final ExecutorService executor;
20        private final AtomicLong rejectedCount = new AtomicLong(0);
21
22        public Bulkhead(String name, int maxConcurrent, int queueSize) {
23            this.name = name;
24            this.semaphore = new Semaphore(maxConcurrent);
25            this.executor = new ThreadPoolExecutor(
26                maxConcurrent, maxConcurrent,
27                0L, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS,
28                new ArrayBlockingQueue<>(queueSize),
29                new ThreadPoolExecutor.AbortPolicy()
30            );
31        }
32
33        public <T> T execute(Callable<T> task, long timeoutMs) throws Exception {
34            if (!semaphore.tryAcquire(timeoutMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)) {
35                rejectedCount.incrementAndGet();
36                throw new BulkheadFullException("Bulkhead " + name + " is full");
37            }
38
39            try {
40                Future<T> future = executor.submit(task);
41                return future.get(timeoutMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);
42            } finally {
43                semaphore.release();
44            }
45        }
46
47        public CompletableFuture<Void> executeAsync(Runnable task) {
48            if (!semaphore.tryAcquire()) {
49                rejectedCount.incrementAndGet();
50                CompletableFuture<Void> failed = new CompletableFuture<>();
51                failed.completeExceptionally(
52                    new BulkheadFullException("Bulkhead " + name + " is full"));
53                return failed;
54            }
55
56            return CompletableFuture.runAsync(task, executor)
57                .whenComplete((result, ex) -> semaphore.release());
58        }
59
60        public long getRejectedCount() {
61            return rejectedCount.get();
62        }
63    }
64
65    public static class BulkheadFullException extends RuntimeException {
66        BulkheadFullException(String msg) { super(msg); }
67    }
68}
69
70// Usage example
71public class BulkheadUsageDemo {
72
73    private final BulkheadManager bulkheadManager = new BulkheadManager();
74
75    // Separate bulkheads for different downstream services
76    private final BulkheadManager.Bulkhead paymentBulkhead =
77        bulkheadManager.getBulkhead("payment", 10, 50);
78
79    private final BulkheadManager.Bulkhead inventoryBulkhead =
80        bulkheadManager.getBulkhead("inventory", 20, 100);
81
82    public PaymentResult processPayment(PaymentRequest request) throws Exception {
83        return paymentBulkhead.execute(() -> {
84            // Call payment service
85            return callPaymentService(request);
86        }, 5000);
87    }
88
89    public InventoryResult checkInventory(String productId) throws Exception {
90        return inventoryBulkhead.execute(() -> {
91            // Call inventory service
92            return callInventoryService(productId);
93        }, 3000);
94    }
95
96    private PaymentResult callPaymentService(PaymentRequest req) { return null; }
97    private InventoryResult callInventoryService(String id) { return null; }
98}

4. Hot Key Problem

1import java.util.concurrent.*;
2
3/**
4 * Hot Key Problem: Few keys receive disproportionate traffic.
5 * Solutions: Local cache, key splitting, rate limiting per key.
6 */
7public class HotKeyHandler {
8
9    // Solution 1: Local cache for hot keys
10    private final ConcurrentHashMap<String, CacheEntry> localCache = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
11    private final ConcurrentHashMap<String, AtomicLong> keyAccessCount = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
12    private final long hotKeyThreshold;
13    private final long localCacheTtlMs;
14
15    public HotKeyHandler(long hotKeyThreshold, long localCacheTtlMs) {
16        this.hotKeyThreshold = hotKeyThreshold;
17        this.localCacheTtlMs = localCacheTtlMs;
18    }
19
20    public String get(String key) {
21        // Track access
22        long count = keyAccessCount
23            .computeIfAbsent(key, k -> new AtomicLong(0))
24            .incrementAndGet();
25
26        // Check local cache first for hot keys
27        CacheEntry entry = localCache.get(key);
28        if (entry != null && !entry.isExpired()) {
29            return entry.value;
30        }
31
32        // Fetch from remote cache/database
33        String value = fetchFromRemote(key);
34
35        // Cache locally if hot
36        if (count > hotKeyThreshold) {
37            localCache.put(key, new CacheEntry(value, localCacheTtlMs));
38        }
39
40        return value;
41    }
42
43    private String fetchFromRemote(String key) {
44        // Fetch from Redis/Database
45        return "value_" + key;
46    }
47
48    static class CacheEntry {
49        final String value;
50        final long expiresAt;
51
52        CacheEntry(String value, long ttlMs) {
53            this.value = value;
54            this.expiresAt = System.currentTimeMillis() + ttlMs;
55        }
56
57        boolean isExpired() {
58            return System.currentTimeMillis() > expiresAt;
59        }
60    }
61}
62
63/**
64 * Solution 2: Key splitting for hot keys.
65 * Split hot key into multiple keys to distribute load.
66 */
67public class HotKeySplitter {
68
69    private final int numSplits;
70    private final Set<String> hotKeys;
71    private final Random random = new Random();
72
73    public HotKeySplitter(int numSplits, Set<String> hotKeys) {
74        this.numSplits = numSplits;
75        this.hotKeys = hotKeys;
76    }
77
78    public String getEffectiveKey(String key) {
79        if (hotKeys.contains(key)) {
80            // Split hot key: key -> key_0, key_1, ..., key_N
81            int split = random.nextInt(numSplits);
82            return key + "_" + split;
83        }
84        return key;
85    }
86
87    public void set(String key, String value) {
88        if (hotKeys.contains(key)) {
89            // Write to all splits
90            for (int i = 0; i < numSplits; i++) {
91                String splitKey = key + "_" + i;
92                setInCache(splitKey, value);
93            }
94        } else {
95            setInCache(key, value);
96        }
97    }
98
99    public String get(String key) {
100        String effectiveKey = getEffectiveKey(key);
101        return getFromCache(effectiveKey);
102    }
103
104    private void setInCache(String key, String value) { /* ... */ }
105    private String getFromCache(String key) { return null; }
106}
107
108/**
109 * Solution 3: Rate limiting per key.
110 */
111public class PerKeyRateLimiter {
112
113    private final ConcurrentHashMap<String, TokenBucket> keyLimiters = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
114    private final int tokensPerSecond;
115    private final int bucketSize;
116
117    public PerKeyRateLimiter(int tokensPerSecond, int bucketSize) {
118        this.tokensPerSecond = tokensPerSecond;
119        this.bucketSize = bucketSize;
120    }
121
122    public boolean tryAcquire(String key) {
123        TokenBucket bucket = keyLimiters.computeIfAbsent(key,
124            k -> new TokenBucket(tokensPerSecond, bucketSize));
125        return bucket.tryAcquire();
126    }
127
128    // Cleanup old entries periodically
129    public void cleanup() {
130        long now = System.currentTimeMillis();
131        keyLimiters.entrySet().removeIf(
132            entry -> now - entry.getValue().lastAccess > 60_000);
133    }
134
135    static class TokenBucket {
136        private final int refillRate;
137        private final int capacity;
138        private double tokens;
139        private long lastRefillTime;
140        volatile long lastAccess;
141
142        TokenBucket(int refillRate, int capacity) {
143            this.refillRate = refillRate;
144            this.capacity = capacity;
145            this.tokens = capacity;
146            this.lastRefillTime = System.currentTimeMillis();
147        }
148
149        synchronized boolean tryAcquire() {
150            lastAccess = System.currentTimeMillis();
151            refill();
152
153            if (tokens >= 1) {
154                tokens--;
155                return true;
156            }
157            return false;
158        }
159
160        private void refill() {
161            long now = System.currentTimeMillis();
162            double elapsed = (now - lastRefillTime) / 1000.0;
163            tokens = Math.min(capacity, tokens + elapsed * refillRate);
164            lastRefillTime = now;
165        }
166    }
167}

5. Connection Pool Tuning

1import com.zaxxer.hikari.HikariConfig;
2import com.zaxxer.hikari.HikariDataSource;
3
4/**
5 * HikariCP connection pool tuning for high traffic.
6 */
7public class ConnectionPoolConfig {
8
9    public HikariDataSource createOptimizedPool() {
10        HikariConfig config = new HikariConfig();
11
12        // Basic settings
13        config.setJdbcUrl("jdbc:mysql://localhost:3306/mydb");
14        config.setUsername("user");
15        config.setPassword("password");
16
17        // Pool sizing
18        // Formula: connections = (core_count * 2) + effective_spindle_count
19        // For SSD: connections = (core_count * 2) + 1
20        config.setMaximumPoolSize(20);
21        config.setMinimumIdle(5);
22
23        // Connection timeout (how long to wait for connection from pool)
24        config.setConnectionTimeout(30_000);  // 30 seconds
25
26        // Idle timeout (how long connection can sit idle before being removed)
27        config.setIdleTimeout(600_000);  // 10 minutes
28
29        // Max lifetime (max lifetime of connection in pool)
30        config.setMaxLifetime(1_800_000);  // 30 minutes
31
32        // Validation
33        config.setConnectionTestQuery("SELECT 1");
34        config.setValidationTimeout(5_000);  // 5 seconds
35
36        // Leak detection
37        config.setLeakDetectionThreshold(60_000);  // 1 minute
38
39        // Performance optimizations
40        config.addDataSourceProperty("cachePrepStmts", "true");
41        config.addDataSourceProperty("prepStmtCacheSize", "250");
42        config.addDataSourceProperty("prepStmtCacheSqlLimit", "2048");
43        config.addDataSourceProperty("useServerPrepStmts", "true");
44
45        return new HikariDataSource(config);
46    }
47
48    /**
49     * Monitor connection pool health.
50     */
51    public void monitorPool(HikariDataSource ds) {
52        System.out.println("Active connections: " + ds.getHikariPoolMXBean().getActiveConnections());
53        System.out.println("Idle connections: " + ds.getHikariPoolMXBean().getIdleConnections());
54        System.out.println("Total connections: " + ds.getHikariPoolMXBean().getTotalConnections());
55        System.out.println("Threads waiting: " + ds.getHikariPoolMXBean().getThreadsAwaitingConnection());
56    }
57}

6. Consistent Hashing

1import java.util.*;
2import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentSkipListMap;
3import java.security.MessageDigest;
4
5/**
6 * Consistent Hashing: Distribute load across nodes with minimal redistribution.
7 */
8public class ConsistentHash<T> {
9
10    private final int virtualNodes;
11    private final ConcurrentSkipListMap<Long, T> ring = new ConcurrentSkipListMap<>();
12    private final MessageDigest md;
13
14    public ConsistentHash(int virtualNodes) {
15        this.virtualNodes = virtualNodes;
16        try {
17            this.md = MessageDigest.getInstance("MD5");
18        } catch (Exception e) {
19            throw new RuntimeException(e);
20        }
21    }
22
23    public void addNode(T node) {
24        for (int i = 0; i < virtualNodes; i++) {
25            long hash = hash(node.toString() + "#" + i);
26            ring.put(hash, node);
27        }
28    }
29
30    public void removeNode(T node) {
31        for (int i = 0; i < virtualNodes; i++) {
32            long hash = hash(node.toString() + "#" + i);
33            ring.remove(hash);
34        }
35    }
36
37    public T getNode(String key) {
38        if (ring.isEmpty()) {
39            return null;
40        }
41
42        long hash = hash(key);
43
44        // Find first node with hash >= key hash
45        Map.Entry<Long, T> entry = ring.ceilingEntry(hash);
46
47        // Wrap around if necessary
48        if (entry == null) {
49            entry = ring.firstEntry();
50        }
51
52        return entry.getValue();
53    }
54
55    // Get N nodes for replication
56    public List<T> getNodes(String key, int count) {
57        if (ring.isEmpty()) {
58            return Collections.emptyList();
59        }
60
61        Set<T> nodes = new LinkedHashSet<>();
62        long hash = hash(key);
63
64        // Get nodes starting from the key's position
65        SortedMap<Long, T> tailMap = ring.tailMap(hash);
66        for (T node : tailMap.values()) {
67            nodes.add(node);
68            if (nodes.size() >= count) break;
69        }
70
71        // Wrap around if needed
72        if (nodes.size() < count) {
73            for (T node : ring.values()) {
74                nodes.add(node);
75                if (nodes.size() >= count) break;
76            }
77        }
78
79        return new ArrayList<>(nodes);
80    }
81
82    private long hash(String key) {
83        md.reset();
84        byte[] digest = md.digest(key.getBytes());
85
86        // Use first 8 bytes for long hash
87        long hash = 0;
88        for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++) {
89            hash = (hash << 8) | (digest[i] & 0xFF);
90        }
91        return hash;
92    }
93}
94
95// Usage
96public class ConsistentHashDemo {
97
98    public void demo() {
99        ConsistentHash<String> hash = new ConsistentHash<>(150);  // 150 virtual nodes
100
101        // Add cache servers
102        hash.addNode("cache-1.example.com");
103        hash.addNode("cache-2.example.com");
104        hash.addNode("cache-3.example.com");
105
106        // Get server for a key
107        String server = hash.getNode("user:123");
108        System.out.println("Key 'user:123' maps to: " + server);
109
110        // Simulate node failure - only ~1/N keys need remapping
111        hash.removeNode("cache-2.example.com");
112
113        // Get replicas for redundancy
114        List<String> replicas = hash.getNodes("user:123", 2);
115        System.out.println("Replicas: " + replicas);
116    }
117}

Real-World Scenarios

Scenario 1: High-Concurrency Counter (Like YouTube Views)

1import java.util.concurrent.*;
2import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
3
4/**
5 * Problem: Millions of view increments per second.
6 * Solution: Local counters + periodic flush to database.
7 */
8public class HighConcurrencyCounter {
9
10    // LongAdder is more efficient than AtomicLong under high contention
11    private final ConcurrentHashMap<String, LongAdder> localCounters = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
12    private final ScheduledExecutorService flusher = Executors.newSingleThreadScheduledExecutor();
13    private final DatabaseService db;
14
15    public HighConcurrencyCounter(DatabaseService db) {
16        this.db = db;
17
18        // Flush every 5 seconds
19        flusher.scheduleAtFixedRate(this::flushToDatabase, 5, 5, TimeUnit.SECONDS);
20    }
21
22    public void increment(String key) {
23        localCounters.computeIfAbsent(key, k -> new LongAdder()).increment();
24    }
25
26    public void incrementBy(String key, long delta) {
27        localCounters.computeIfAbsent(key, k -> new LongAdder()).add(delta);
28    }
29
30    public long getApproximateCount(String key) {
31        LongAdder adder = localCounters.get(key);
32        long local = adder != null ? adder.sum() : 0;
33        long persisted = db.getCount(key);
34        return local + persisted;
35    }
36
37    private void flushToDatabase() {
38        // Swap out current counters
39        Map<String, Long> toFlush = new HashMap<>();
40
41        localCounters.forEach((key, adder) -> {
42            long count = adder.sumThenReset();
43            if (count > 0) {
44                toFlush.put(key, count);
45            }
46        });
47
48        if (!toFlush.isEmpty()) {
49            // Batch update to database
50            db.batchIncrement(toFlush);
51        }
52    }
53
54    public void shutdown() {
55        flusher.shutdown();
56        flushToDatabase();  // Final flush
57    }
58
59    interface DatabaseService {
60        long getCount(String key);
61        void batchIncrement(Map<String, Long> increments);
62    }
63}

Scenario 2: Distributed Rate Limiter

1import redis.clients.jedis.Jedis;
2import java.util.*;
3
4/**
5 * Problem: Rate limit across multiple server instances.
6 * Solution: Sliding window with Redis.
7 */
8public class DistributedRateLimiter {
9
10    private final Jedis redis;
11    private final int maxRequests;
12    private final long windowSizeMs;
13
14    public DistributedRateLimiter(Jedis redis, int maxRequests, long windowSizeMs) {
15        this.redis = redis;
16        this.maxRequests = maxRequests;
17        this.windowSizeMs = windowSizeMs;
18    }
19
20    // Sliding window using sorted set
21    public boolean tryAcquire(String userId) {
22        String key = "ratelimit:" + userId;
23        long now = System.currentTimeMillis();
24        long windowStart = now - windowSizeMs;
25
26        // Lua script for atomic operation
27        String script =
28            "local key = KEYS[1]\n" +
29            "local now = tonumber(ARGV[1])\n" +
30            "local window_start = tonumber(ARGV[2])\n" +
31            "local max_requests = tonumber(ARGV[3])\n" +
32            "local window_size = tonumber(ARGV[4])\n" +
33            "\n" +
34            "-- Remove old entries\n" +
35            "redis.call('ZREMRANGEBYSCORE', key, '-inf', window_start)\n" +
36            "\n" +
37            "-- Count current requests\n" +
38            "local count = redis.call('ZCARD', key)\n" +
39            "\n" +
40            "if count < max_requests then\n" +
41            "    -- Add new request\n" +
42            "    redis.call('ZADD', key, now, now .. ':' .. math.random())\n" +
43            "    redis.call('PEXPIRE', key, window_size)\n" +
44            "    return 1\n" +
45            "else\n" +
46            "    return 0\n" +
47            "end";
48
49        Long result = (Long) redis.eval(
50            script,
51            Collections.singletonList(key),
52            Arrays.asList(
53                String.valueOf(now),
54                String.valueOf(windowStart),
55                String.valueOf(maxRequests),
56                String.valueOf(windowSizeMs)
57            )
58        );
59
60        return result == 1;
61    }
62
63    // Get remaining requests
64    public int getRemainingRequests(String userId) {
65        String key = "ratelimit:" + userId;
66        long windowStart = System.currentTimeMillis() - windowSizeMs;
67
68        redis.zremrangeByScore(key, "-inf", String.valueOf(windowStart));
69        long count = redis.zcard(key);
70
71        return Math.max(0, maxRequests - (int) count);
72    }
73}

Scenario 3: Eventual Consistency with Conflict Resolution

1import java.util.*;
2
3/**
4 * Problem: Multiple services may update same data concurrently.
5 * Solution: Last-write-wins with vector clocks for conflict detection.
6 */
7public class EventualConsistencyStore {
8
9    private final ConcurrentHashMap<String, VersionedValue> store = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
10
11    static class VectorClock {
12        private final Map<String, Long> clock = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
13
14        public void increment(String nodeId) {
15            clock.merge(nodeId, 1L, Long::sum);
16        }
17
18        public void merge(VectorClock other) {
19            for (Map.Entry<String, Long> entry : other.clock.entrySet()) {
20                clock.merge(entry.getKey(), entry.getValue(), Math::max);
21            }
22        }
23
24        public boolean happensBefore(VectorClock other) {
25            boolean atLeastOneLess = false;
26
27            for (String node : getAllNodes(other)) {
28                long thisValue = clock.getOrDefault(node, 0L);
29                long otherValue = other.clock.getOrDefault(node, 0L);
30
31                if (thisValue > otherValue) {
32                    return false;  // Not strictly before
33                }
34                if (thisValue < otherValue) {
35                    atLeastOneLess = true;
36                }
37            }
38
39            return atLeastOneLess;
40        }
41
42        public boolean isConcurrent(VectorClock other) {
43            return !happensBefore(other) && !other.happensBefore(this);
44        }
45
46        private Set<String> getAllNodes(VectorClock other) {
47            Set<String> nodes = new HashSet<>(clock.keySet());
48            nodes.addAll(other.clock.keySet());
49            return nodes;
50        }
51
52        public VectorClock copy() {
53            VectorClock copy = new VectorClock();
54            copy.clock.putAll(this.clock);
55            return copy;
56        }
57    }
58
59    static class VersionedValue {
60        final String value;
61        final VectorClock clock;
62        final long timestamp;
63
64        VersionedValue(String value, VectorClock clock) {
65            this.value = value;
66            this.clock = clock;
67            this.timestamp = System.currentTimeMillis();
68        }
69    }
70
71    public void put(String key, String value, String nodeId) {
72        store.compute(key, (k, existing) -> {
73            VectorClock newClock;
74
75            if (existing == null) {
76                newClock = new VectorClock();
77            } else {
78                newClock = existing.clock.copy();
79            }
80
81            newClock.increment(nodeId);
82            return new VersionedValue(value, newClock);
83        });
84    }
85
86    public String get(String key) {
87        VersionedValue v = store.get(key);
88        return v != null ? v.value : null;
89    }
90
91    // Handle incoming replication
92    public ConflictResult replicate(String key, String value, VectorClock incomingClock) {
93        return store.compute(key, (k, existing) -> {
94            if (existing == null) {
95                return new VersionedValue(value, incomingClock);
96            }
97
98            if (incomingClock.happensBefore(existing.clock)) {
99                // Incoming is older - keep existing
100                return existing;
101            }
102
103            if (existing.clock.happensBefore(incomingClock)) {
104                // Incoming is newer - accept it
105                return new VersionedValue(value, incomingClock);
106            }
107
108            // Concurrent - conflict! Use LWW (last-write-wins) or merge
109            VersionedValue winner = existing.timestamp > 0 ? existing :
110                new VersionedValue(value, incomingClock);
111
112            // Merge clocks
113            winner.clock.merge(incomingClock);
114
115            return winner;
116        }) != null ? ConflictResult.ACCEPTED : ConflictResult.REJECTED;
117    }
118
119    enum ConflictResult { ACCEPTED, REJECTED, CONFLICT }
120}

Common Interview Questions

Q1: How would you implement a thread-safe LRU cache?

1import java.util.*;
2import java.util.concurrent.locks.*;
3
4public class ConcurrentLRUCache<K, V> {
5
6    private final int capacity;
7    private final Map<K, Node<K, V>> map;
8    private final Node<K, V> head, tail;
9    private final ReentrantReadWriteLock lock = new ReentrantReadWriteLock();
10
11    static class Node<K, V> {
12        K key;
13        V value;
14        Node<K, V> prev, next;
15
16        Node(K key, V value) {
17            this.key = key;
18            this.value = value;
19        }
20    }
21
22    public ConcurrentLRUCache(int capacity) {
23        this.capacity = capacity;
24        this.map = new HashMap<>();
25        this.head = new Node<>(null, null);
26        this.tail = new Node<>(null, null);
27        head.next = tail;
28        tail.prev = head;
29    }
30
31    public V get(K key) {
32        lock.readLock().lock();
33        try {
34            Node<K, V> node = map.get(key);
35            if (node == null) return null;
36
37            // Need write lock to move to front
38            lock.readLock().unlock();
39            lock.writeLock().lock();
40            try {
41                moveToFront(node);
42                return node.value;
43            } finally {
44                lock.writeLock().unlock();
45                lock.readLock().lock();
46            }
47        } finally {
48            lock.readLock().unlock();
49        }
50    }
51
52    public void put(K key, V value) {
53        lock.writeLock().lock();
54        try {
55            Node<K, V> node = map.get(key);
56
57            if (node != null) {
58                node.value = value;
59                moveToFront(node);
60            } else {
61                node = new Node<>(key, value);
62                map.put(key, node);
63                addToFront(node);
64
65                if (map.size() > capacity) {
66                    Node<K, V> lru = tail.prev;
67                    remove(lru);
68                    map.remove(lru.key);
69                }
70            }
71        } finally {
72            lock.writeLock().unlock();
73        }
74    }
75
76    private void moveToFront(Node<K, V> node) {
77        remove(node);
78        addToFront(node);
79    }
80
81    private void remove(Node<K, V> node) {
82        node.prev.next = node.next;
83        node.next.prev = node.prev;
84    }
85
86    private void addToFront(Node<K, V> node) {
87        node.next = head.next;
88        node.prev = head;
89        head.next.prev = node;
90        head.next = node;
91    }
92}

Q2: Explain deadlock vs livelock vs starvation.

Condition Deadlock Livelock Starvation
Definition Threads blocked waiting for each other Threads active but no progress Thread never gets resources
State Blocked Running Waiting
Resource usage Holding resources Releasing/acquiring Never acquiring
Recovery Needs intervention May resolve itself May resolve with fairness
Example A waits for B, B waits for A Both back off and retry simultaneously Low priority thread never scheduled 
1// Deadlock example
2Object lock1 = new Object();
3Object lock2 = new Object();
4
5// Thread 1: synchronized(lock1) { synchronized(lock2) { } }
6// Thread 2: synchronized(lock2) { synchronized(lock1) { } }
7
8// Livelock example: Two people in corridor both moving same direction
9AtomicBoolean resourceAvailable = new AtomicBoolean(true);
10// Thread 1: while(!canProceed) { releaseResource(); waitRandom(); tryAcquire(); }
11// Thread 2: Same pattern - both keep backing off
12
13// Starvation: Non-fair lock with many high-priority threads
14ReentrantLock unfairLock = new ReentrantLock(false);  // unfair
15// Low priority thread may never acquire lock

Q3: How to prevent database connection pool exhaustion?

  1. Proper pool sizing: connections = (core_count * 2) + effective_spindle_count
  2. Connection timeout: Fail fast instead of waiting indefinitely
  3. Leak detection: Log connections held too long
  4. Statement timeout: Kill long-running queries
  5. Circuit breaker: Stop trying when database is overwhelmed
  6. Read replicas: Distribute read load
  7. Caching: Reduce database calls

Q4: When to use pessimistic vs optimistic locking?

Scenario Use Pessimistic Use Optimistic
Conflict probability High Low
Read:Write ratio Write-heavy Read-heavy
Transaction duration Short Can be long
Retry cost High Low
Example Bank transfer Profile update

Q5: How does ConcurrentHashMap achieve thread safety without locking entire map?

  • Segment locking (Java 7): Map divided into segments, each with own lock
  • CAS + synchronized (Java 8+): Lock-free reads, fine-grained locking for writes
  • Node-level locking: Only lock the bucket being modified
  • Volatile reads: Reads see latest writes without locking

Summary: Quick Reference

Problem Solution
CPU-intensive parallel work Fork/Join Framework
Lock-free data structures CAS with AtomicReference
ABA problem AtomicStampedReference
Reduce lock contention Lock striping
Distributed coordination ZooKeeper / Redlock
Thundering herd Request coalescing
System overload Load shedding
Cascading failures Bulkhead pattern
Hot keys Local cache + key splitting
Node failures Consistent hashing
Concurrent modification Optimistic locking (version)
Exclusive access Pessimistic locking (SELECT FOR UPDATE)

References

  • Java Concurrency in Practice - Brian Goetz
  • The Art of Multiprocessor Programming
  • Martin Kleppmann - Designing Data-Intensive Applications
  • Redis Distributed Locks (Redlock)
  • HikariCP - Connection Pool
